title: HTTP Methods: DELETE and When to Use It

content.problemStatement:
In this lesson, we'll explore the DELETE HTTP method and understand when to use it in your Flask applications.

Key points about the DELETE method:

1. Purpose: It's used to request the removal of a resource from the server.

2. No request body: Typically, DELETE requests don't have a request body. The resource to be deleted is usually specified in the URL.

When to use DELETE:
- Removing a user account
- Deleting a blog post
- Removing an item from a database

In Flask, you can handle DELETE requests using the @app.route decorator with the methods parameter:

```python
from flask import Flask

app = Flask(__name__)

@app.route('/user/', methods=['DELETE'])
def delete_user(user_id):
    # Code to delete the user with the given user_id
    return f"User {user_id} has been deleted", 200
```

In this example, when a DELETE request is made to a URL like '/user/123', Flask will call the delete_user function, passing 123 as the user_id parameter.

### NOTE:
GET requests can be easily made through a web browser's address bar, DELETE requests typically require special tools (like cURL) or JavaScript in a web application to be executed.
